name: Improve GainForest technical SEO

description: Baseline SEO audit setup; deterministic audit found 8 technical SEO findings, but check-site-meta smoke hung past timeout.

asi.hypothesis: Establish a measurable SEO proxy using a deterministic local audit plus npx check-site-meta readiness smoke.
asi.finding: Baseline has missing branded descriptions in all locales except page metadata, no root hreflang alternates, and no Organization/WebSite JSON-LD sameAs entity graph.
asi.benchmark_issue: The first autoresearch.sh invocation timed out because the interactive check-site-meta UI did not terminate cleanly inside command substitution.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix autoresearch.sh to bound and clean up check-site-meta, then address branded descriptions, hreflang alternates, and JSON-LD.

description: Fix check-site-meta smoke so the SEO benchmark terminates cleanly and records readiness.

asi.hypothesis: Bounding the interactive npx check-site-meta UI will make the autoresearch loop usable while preserving the requested metadata preview tool.
asi.finding: check-site-meta boots successfully in agent mode and reports Ready; primary SEO findings remain 8 before product SEO changes.
asi.next_action_hint: Now reduce the 8 findings by adding branded localized descriptions, hreflang alternates, and Organization/WebSite JSON-LD.

description: Add localized brand SEO descriptions, home hreflang alternates, and Organization/WebSite JSON-LD.

asi.hypothesis: Strengthening brand/entity signals and hreflang coverage will eliminate the technical SEO findings most likely to affect direct GainForest search presentation.
asi.finding: The audit dropped from 8 findings to 0 after updating all localized root descriptions, adding language alternates to root metadata, and injecting translated Organization/WebSite JSON-LD with sameAs links.
asi.tradeoff: One non-blocking warning remains because the technical audit is clean and future experiments should target content depth, internal links, and Search Console data.
asi.next_action_hint: Validate TypeScript/i18n, then consider a second SEO experiment that adds richer crawlable internal links or page-specific metadata for important public pages.

name: Improve localized public-page SEO metadata

description: Baseline localized public-page metadata audit with 12 gaps across Projects and Observations.

asi.hypothesis: After core technical SEO is clean, localized metadata for public listing pages is the next measurable SEO lever.
asi.finding: Projects and Observations currently have hardcoded or missing localized metadata; projects translations lack GainForest in descriptions, and observations metadata translations are absent across all locales.
asi.next_action_hint: Convert Projects and Observations pages to generateMetadata with next-intl, add brand-forward metadata translations for en/es/pt/sw/id, then rerun.

description: Localize Projects and Observations metadata with GainForest-forward titles and descriptions.

asi.hypothesis: Localized, brand-forward Metadata API values for high-value public listing pages improve index snippets and reinforce GainForest entity relevance beyond the home page.
asi.finding: public_metadata_gaps dropped from 12 to 0 by moving Projects and Observations to generateMetadata and adding en/es/pt/sw/id metadata translations that name GainForest.
asi.tradeoff: Metadata copy is intentionally plain and brand-forward, avoiding protocol jargon and keeping all configured languages in sync.
asi.next_action_hint: Run validation; then consider another SEO phase for sitemap breadth or dynamic project-page structured data.

name: Improve public page hreflang metadata

description: Baseline public hreflang audit; 10 public pages set canonicals without localized alternates.

asi.hypothesis: Page-level metadata with alternates.canonical may override root language alternates, so high-value public pages need localized hreflang alternates directly in their metadata.
asi.finding: 10 sitemap-backed public pages currently lack localizedAlternates in page metadata: home, observations, projects, organizations, bioblitz, grants, devices, status, privacy, and docs/lexicons.
asi.benchmark_note: The new audit checks a real SEO invariant: when page metadata defines alternates, it should keep language variants so Google can cluster localized URLs.
asi.next_action_hint: Add a shared localizedAlternates helper and use it in the 10 public page metadata definitions.

description: Add shared localizedAlternates helper and apply hreflang alternates to public page metadata.

asi.hypothesis: Preserving hreflang alternates on page-level metadata helps Google cluster localized public pages instead of seeing only canonical URLs from child metadata.
asi.finding: public_hreflang_gaps dropped from 10 to 0 after adding localizedAlternates and applying it to home, observations, projects, organizations, bioblitz, grants, devices, status, privacy, and docs/lexicons.
asi.tradeoff: No new user-facing copy was added; this centralizes metadata alternates without changing visible UI or route behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript/i18n validation, then consider a future phase for dynamic project/organization detail pages or richer sitemap lastModified signals.

name: Improve dynamic project detail SEO metadata

description: Baseline dynamic project detail metadata audit; project pages lack hreflang, OG URL, and Twitter card metadata.

asi.hypothesis: Sitemap-backed dynamic project detail pages are high-value SEO landing pages, so their metadata should preserve localized alternates and complete social preview fields.
asi.finding: The dynamic project detail page currently has three metadata gaps: no localized alternates, no canonical Open Graph URL, and no Twitter/X card metadata.
asi.benchmark_note: This is not benchmark overfitting: the audited fields correspond to standard search/social metadata for dynamic pages already included in the sitemap.
asi.next_action_hint: Update app/projects/[did]/[rkey]/page.tsx to use localizedAlternates(localProjectHref(urlIdentifier, rkey)), set openGraph.url, and add a summary_large_image Twitter card.

description: Complete dynamic project detail metadata with hreflang alternates, canonical OG URL, and Twitter card previews.

asi.hypothesis: Completing metadata on sitemap-backed project detail pages will improve localized indexing and link-preview consistency for the content most likely to rank beyond the home page.
asi.finding: dynamic_detail_metadata_gaps dropped from 3 to 0 after adding localizedAlternates, openGraph.url, and summary_large_image Twitter metadata using each project's title, description, and image.
asi.tradeoff: The change reuses existing project copy and images, adds no keyword stuffing or new UI text, and keeps pending/unindexed project pages unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Validate TypeScript; future iterations could examine organization/account detail metadata or live Search Console data if available.

name: Improve public account profile SEO metadata

description: Baseline account/profile metadata audit; public profiles lack hreflang, OG URL, and Twitter card metadata.

asi.hypothesis: Public organization and user profiles are linked across GainForest and can act as SEO landing pages, so they should preserve localized alternates and complete social preview metadata.
asi.finding: The public account layout currently has three metadata gaps: no localized alternates, no canonical Open Graph URL, and no Twitter/X card metadata.
asi.benchmark_note: This extends the same standard metadata coverage from indexed project pages to public profile pages without changing visible content or adding keywords.
asi.next_action_hint: Update app/account/[did]/layout.tsx to compute an accountHref, use localizedAlternates, and add Open Graph/Twitter preview metadata from the account name, description, and avatar.

description: Complete public account/profile metadata with hreflang alternates, canonical OG URL, and Twitter card previews.

asi.hypothesis: Adding localized alternates and social preview fields to public profiles will improve how organization/user profile pages cluster in search and render when shared, without changing content or adding keyword stuffing.
asi.finding: account_profile_metadata_gaps dropped from 3 to 0 after account metadata started using localizedAlternates plus Open Graph/Twitter preview metadata based on the existing account name, description, and avatar.
asi.tradeoff: This keeps existing fallback copy unchanged and introduces no new visible UI text; profile-not-found metadata remains noindex.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript/i18n validation; future iterations could focus on sitemap discovery for public organization profiles or richer lastModified signals.

name: Improve organization profile sitemap discovery

description: Baseline sitemap discovery audit; public certified organization profiles are not discovered through sitemap.

asi.hypothesis: Now that account/profile metadata is complete, crawler discovery is the next bottleneck: public organization profile landing pages should appear in sitemap.xml alongside project pages.
asi.finding: The sitemap currently includes static sections and project detail pages, but does not query certified organization records or emit /account/{did} organization profile URLs.
asi.benchmark_note: This is a real SEO discovery issue, not benchmark gaming: XML sitemaps are the canonical way to expose crawlable dynamic URLs that may otherwise only be reachable through paginated/client-heavy surfaces.
asi.next_action_hint: Add a best-effort appCertifiedActorOrganization sitemap query and localized /account/{did} entries with weekly priority.

description: Add best-effort certified organization profile URLs to sitemap for crawler discovery.

asi.hypothesis: Public organization profile pages now have complete metadata, so adding them to sitemap.xml will improve crawler discovery of important GainForest entity pages.
asi.finding: sitemap_discovery_gaps dropped from 2 to 0 by querying appCertifiedActorOrganization and emitting localized /account/{did} sitemap entries with weekly change frequency.
asi.tradeoff: The query is best-effort and keeps the existing static/project sitemap fallback; it skips private/hidden and obvious test/demo/sample organization names to avoid promoting non-public or disposable profiles.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript validation; future iterations could add account canonical handle resolution if a cheap handle source becomes available, or improve lastModified for projects/profiles from update timestamps.

description: Fix sitemap organization profile type narrowing while preserving sitemap discovery score.

asi.hypothesis: A proper TypeScript type predicate keeps the organization sitemap implementation type-safe without changing SEO behavior.
asi.finding: The SEO metric stayed at 0 gaps while narrowing shouldIncludeOrganizationProfile to guarantee node.did is a string for encodeURIComponent.
asi.reason_for_keep_despite_equal_primary: This is a correctness repair for the previously kept sitemap implementation; discarding would leave the branch with a TypeScript error.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript/i18n validation now; consider adding autoresearch.checks.sh in a future loop so type errors gate keep/discard automatically.

name: Improve localized static page metadata

description: Baseline localized static metadata audit; home, devices, and status still serve hardcoded or missing localized metadata.

asi.hypothesis: After adding hreflang across public pages, localized URLs should also have localized titles/descriptions; otherwise Google may see English snippets on non-English pages.
asi.finding: 13 localized static metadata gaps: home/devices/status pages use static metadata instead of getTranslations, and devices/status metadata translations are missing for all configured locales.
asi.benchmark_note: This is a real SEO/i18n issue, not benchmark gaming: metadata shown to crawlers should match the localized URL language.
asi.next_action_hint: Convert home/devices/status to generateMetadata and add common.devices.metadata and common.status.metadata translations in en/es/pt/sw/id.

description: Localize home, devices, and status metadata using translated Metadata API values.

asi.hypothesis: Localized public URLs should not serve hardcoded English metadata; converting static metadata to getTranslations improves snippet language alignment for crawlers.
asi.finding: localized_static_metadata_gaps dropped from 13 to 0 after home, devices, and status moved to generateMetadata and devices/status translations were added for en/es/pt/sw/id.
asi.secondary_metric_note: seo_findings rose to 2 because the older home metadata audit still expects static English text in app/page.tsx; the implementation now gets equivalent localized values from common.seo.
asi.tradeoff: Adds translations only for metadata, with no new visible UI copy or keyword stuffing.
asi.next_action_hint: Update the audit to recognize translated home metadata as satisfying the home title/description brand invariant, then rerun.

name: Restore core SEO audit after localized metadata

description: Baseline core SEO audit after localizing home metadata; stale audit reports two false home metadata findings.

asi.hypothesis: After home metadata moved from static English constants to translated common.seo values, the core SEO audit should be aligned to the Metadata API pattern instead of treating localization as a regression.
asi.finding: Baseline seo_findings is 2 solely from home-metadata-description-brand and home-metadata-title-brand checks that look for literal English in app/page.tsx.
asi.benchmark_note: The underlying common.seo translations still include GainForest and satisfy length/brand checks; the remaining issue is audit coverage of localized generateMetadata.
asi.next_action_hint: Change the home metadata audit to accept getTranslations("common.seo") plus the already validated common.seo messages.

description: Align core SEO audit with localized home Metadata API usage.

asi.hypothesis: The audit should validate the real SEO invariant—home metadata uses branded common.seo translations—rather than requiring hardcoded English in app/page.tsx.
asi.finding: seo_findings dropped from 2 to 0 after the home metadata checks accepted getTranslations("common.seo"), whose localized messages are already validated for GainForest branding and length.
asi.tradeoff: This is an audit correctness change, not a relaxation: common.seo translations remain checked for brand presence and suitable lengths in every configured language.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript/i18n validation; future phases can focus on noindex/robots for private app routes or live Search Console signals.

name: Improve remaining indexable page hreflang

description: Baseline remaining indexable-page hreflang audit; feed, submit-data, and Tainá lack localized alternates.

asi.hypothesis: Indexable public pages that define page-level canonical metadata should also preserve localized hreflang alternates so Google can cluster their localized versions.
asi.finding: Three indexable pages still use plain alternates.canonical without localized alternates: /feed, /submit-data, and /taina.
asi.benchmark_note: This extends an already-used SEO invariant to remaining indexable pages and skips routes that explicitly set noindex, avoiding benchmark gaming or blanket changes to private pages.
asi.next_action_hint: Change feed, submit-data, and Tainá metadata to use localizedAlternates for their canonical paths.

description: Preserve hreflang alternates for feed, submit-data, and Tainá metadata.

asi.hypothesis: Using localizedAlternates on remaining indexable public pages will keep canonical and hreflang metadata consistent across all localized variants.
asi.finding: indexable_page_hreflang_gaps dropped from 3 to 0 after /feed, /submit-data, and /taina switched from bare alternates.canonical to localizedAlternates.
asi.tradeoff: No user-facing copy changed, and no private/noindex pages were made indexable; the change only preserves language alternates for pages already indexable.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript/i18n validation; future phases could decide whether /feed, /submit-data, and /taina should also be included in sitemap or explicitly noindexed based on product SEO strategy.

name: Improve public landing page sitemap discovery

description: Baseline public landing-page sitemap audit; submit-data and Tainá landing pages are missing from sitemap.

asi.hypothesis: Public landing/setup pages with localized metadata should be discoverable in sitemap.xml if they remain indexable, especially /submit-data for field partners and /taina for the field assistant.
asi.finding: Two sitemap discovery gaps remain: /submit-data and /taina are indexable public landing pages with localized metadata/hreflang but are not listed in ROUTES for sitemap generation.
asi.benchmark_note: This focuses on durable landing/setup pages rather than adding every app page; /feed remains a separate product-strategy decision because it is dynamic and potentially lower SEO value.
asi.next_action_hint: Add /submit-data and /taina to sitemap ROUTES with moderate weekly priorities, then rerun.

description: Add submit-data and Tainá public landing pages to sitemap routes.

asi.hypothesis: Adding durable public landing/setup pages to sitemap.xml will improve crawler discovery for field-partner data submissions and Tainá without broadening the sitemap to low-value dynamic app surfaces.
asi.finding: public_landing_sitemap_gaps dropped from 2 to 0 after adding /submit-data and /taina to ROUTES with weekly change frequency and moderate priority.
asi.tradeoff: Did not add /feed to the sitemap because its SEO value is less clear and it may be more of a dynamic app surface; kept the change focused on stable landing pages with localized metadata.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript/i18n validation. Future iterations can decide whether /feed should be noindexed or intentionally listed based on Search Console/product strategy.

name: Decide public feed sitemap strategy

description: Baseline feed sitemap strategy audit; public feed is indexable with hreflang metadata but absent from sitemap.

asi.hypothesis: The public activity feed is server-rendered and indexable, so it needs an explicit sitemap/noindex strategy; listing it may help crawlers discover fresh public activity and internal links.
asi.finding: feed_sitemap_gaps baseline is 1 because /feed has localized metadata and no noindex robots directive but is not listed in sitemap ROUTES.
asi.benchmark_note: This does not blindly add every app route; the audit flags only /feed because it is already indexable and server-renders public activity.
asi.next_action_hint: Add /feed to sitemap ROUTES with daily change frequency and moderate priority, rather than noindexing it, because its public activity content can support discovery.

description: Add public activity feed to sitemap with daily change frequency.

asi.hypothesis: Listing /feed in sitemap.xml improves discovery of fresh public GainForest activity and the internal links it exposes, while preserving its existing indexable status.
asi.finding: feed_sitemap_gaps dropped from 1 to 0 after adding /feed to ROUTES with daily change frequency and moderate priority.
asi.tradeoff: Chose sitemap inclusion rather than noindex because the feed server-renders public activity and can help crawlers discover recent projects, observations, and profiles; did not broaden the sitemap to private or noindex app routes.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript/i18n validation; future SEO phases can focus on structured data for list pages or Search Console-informed decisions.

name: Improve public list page structured data

description: Baseline list-page structured data audit; projects, observations, and organizations lack CollectionPage JSON-LD.

asi.hypothesis: Public listing pages with localized metadata should expose CollectionPage JSON-LD so crawlers can understand they are index pages for projects, observations, and organizations.
asi.finding: list_structured_data_gaps baseline is 3: /projects, /observations, and /organizations have strong metadata and sitemap coverage but no CollectionPage structured data.
asi.benchmark_note: This is a standard structured-data improvement for page type/entity understanding, not keyword stuffing or benchmark gaming.
asi.next_action_hint: Add localized CollectionPage JSON-LD to the three server page wrappers using existing metadata translations and absolute canonical URLs.

description: Add localized CollectionPage JSON-LD to public listing pages.

asi.hypothesis: Adding CollectionPage JSON-LD to major public listing pages will improve page-type/entity understanding for search crawlers without changing visible copy or stuffing keywords.
asi.finding: list_structured_data_gaps dropped from 3 to 0 after /projects, /observations, and /organizations emitted localized CollectionPage JSON-LD with absolute canonical URLs and WebSite parent links.
asi.tradeoff: The pages now await translations and request origin in their server wrappers, but they already used translated metadata and the heavy list UI remains behind existing Suspense boundaries.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript/i18n validation. Future iterations can consider ItemList structured data only if actual server-side list items are available without extra heavy fetching.

name: Improve public list page social metadata

description: Baseline list-page social metadata audit; projects, observations, and organizations lack page-specific OG/Twitter metadata.

asi.hypothesis: Major public listing pages should have page-specific Open Graph/Twitter metadata so check-site-meta and shared links show the relevant list page, not generic home/site metadata.
asi.finding: list_social_metadata_gaps baseline is 3: /projects, /observations, and /organizations have localized title/description and CollectionPage JSON-LD but no page-specific openGraph/twitter metadata.
asi.benchmark_note: This is a standard social/metadata preview improvement and aligns with the user's check-site-meta requirement; it does not alter visible UI or keyword density.
asi.next_action_hint: Add openGraph and twitter metadata to generateMetadata for the three list pages using existing localized metadata and the shared OG image.

description: Add page-specific Open Graph and Twitter metadata to public listing pages.

asi.hypothesis: Page-specific Open Graph/Twitter metadata on major list pages will make check-site-meta/social previews reflect the relevant public list page rather than generic home metadata.
asi.finding: list_social_metadata_gaps dropped from 3 to 0 after /projects, /observations, and /organizations added localized OG/Twitter title/description, canonical URL, and the existing GainForest OG image.
asi.tradeoff: No visible copy changed; the metadata reuses existing translations and assets. The shared OG image is generic GainForest art, but page-specific titles/descriptions prevent generic previews.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript/i18n validation. Future iterations can consider similar social metadata for feed, submit-data, Tainá, devices, and status if they remain indexable.

name: Improve remaining public page social metadata

description: Baseline remaining social metadata audit; feed, submit-data, Tainá, devices, and status lack page-specific OG/Twitter previews.

asi.hypothesis: Remaining indexable public pages with localized metadata and sitemap/hreflang coverage should also have page-specific social preview metadata for check-site-meta and shared links.
asi.finding: remaining_social_metadata_gaps baseline is 5: /feed, /submit-data, /taina, /devices, and /status do not use page-specific Open Graph/Twitter metadata.
asi.benchmark_note: This follows the same preview metadata standard applied to list pages and only targets public/indexable pages, not private/noindex routes.
asi.next_action_hint: Add a reusable socialPreviewMetadata helper and use it in those five generateMetadata functions.

description: Add reusable social preview metadata to remaining indexable public pages.

asi.hypothesis: Adding page-specific Open Graph/Twitter metadata to remaining public/indexable pages will make check-site-meta and shared previews reflect each page instead of falling back to generic site metadata.
asi.finding: remaining_social_metadata_gaps dropped from 5 to 0 after adding a reusable socialPreviewMetadata helper and applying it to /feed, /submit-data, /taina, /devices, and /status.
asi.tradeoff: No user-facing copy changed; metadata reuses existing localized titles/descriptions and the existing GainForest OG image. The helper also reduces future drift across pages.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript/i18n validation. Future iterations can target richer content/internal-link signals or consolidate duplicate social metadata usage on list pages with the new helper.

name: Improve project detail breadcrumb structured data

description: Baseline project breadcrumb audit; dynamic project detail pages lack BreadcrumbList JSON-LD.

asi.hypothesis: Dynamic project detail pages are high-value sitemap-backed landing pages, so BreadcrumbList JSON-LD can help search engines understand the home → projects → project hierarchy.
asi.finding: project_breadcrumb_gaps baseline is 1 because app/projects/[did]/[rkey]/page.tsx has strong metadata and project JSON-LD via the Cert view but no BreadcrumbList JSON-LD.
asi.benchmark_note: BreadcrumbList is standard structured data for search-result hierarchy and does not alter visible UI, keyword density, or crawler access.
asi.next_action_hint: Add buildProjectBreadcrumbJsonLd and emit a project-breadcrumb-json-ld script for both Cert-backed and fallback project detail renders.

description: Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD to dynamic project detail pages.

asi.hypothesis: BreadcrumbList JSON-LD on sitemap-backed project detail pages will help search engines understand the hierarchy from GainForest to the projects index to each project page.
asi.finding: project_breadcrumb_gaps dropped from 1 to 0 after adding buildProjectBreadcrumbJsonLd and a project-breadcrumb-json-ld script to both Cert-backed and fallback project detail renders.
asi.tradeoff: No visible UI copy changed; breadcrumb labels reuse existing localized project-page copy and canonical project URLs. Pending/unindexed publishing pages remain unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript/i18n validation. Future iterations can consider consolidating social metadata helpers on list pages or adding breadcrumbs to account/profile pages if useful.

name: Improve public account profile structured data

description: Baseline account/profile structured data audit; public profile pages lack ProfilePage JSON-LD.

asi.hypothesis: Public account/profile pages already have localized metadata, social previews, hreflang, and organization sitemap discovery, so ProfilePage JSON-LD can add entity understanding for profiles without changing visible content.
asi.finding: account_profile_structured_data_gaps baseline is 1 because app/account/[did]/layout.tsx renders public profile metadata and UI but no ProfilePage structured data with Person/Organization mainEntity fields.
asi.benchmark_note: ProfilePage plus Person/Organization is standard schema.org structured data for public profile/entity pages; the audit does not require adding keywords or exposing technical identifiers.
asi.next_action_hint: Add buildAccountProfileJsonLd using the account display name, description, avatar, website/social links, canonical profile URL, and Person vs Organization based on account.kind; emit an account-profile-json-ld script in AccountLayout.

description: Add ProfilePage JSON-LD to public account/profile pages.

asi.hypothesis: ProfilePage JSON-LD with Person/Organization mainEntity data will improve crawler understanding of public GainForest profile pages after metadata, hreflang, social previews, and organization sitemap discovery are already in place.
asi.finding: account_profile_structured_data_gaps dropped from 1 to 0 after adding buildAccountProfileJsonLd and an account-profile-json-ld script in app/account/[did]/layout.tsx.
asi.tradeoff: No visible UI copy changed. Structured data uses existing profile fields, omits DIDs/handles as identifiers, validates URLs before using website/social/avatar fields, and switches mainEntity type based on account.kind.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript validation. Future SEO iterations could add Organization/Person-specific optional fields only if reliable source data exists, or move to richer internal-link/content signals.

name: Improve observations server-rendered crawlable content

description: Baseline observations crawlable-content audit; observations index lacks an initial server-rendered sightings page.

asi.hypothesis: After metadata, hreflang, sitemap, social previews, and CollectionPage JSON-LD are clean, the observations index should expose real public sighting cards in the initial server HTML instead of relying entirely on client-side fetching.
asi.finding: observations_server_content_gaps baseline is 1 because app/observations/page.tsx renders RecordExplorer without an initialPage, so crawlers/no-JS previews get metadata and JSON-LD but not the first page of observation content.
asi.benchmark_note: This measures crawler-visible content depth for an already indexable public listing page; it does not reward keyword stuffing, hidden content, or changing robots rules.
asi.next_action_hint: Fetch the default first image-sighting page with walkOccurrences in the server page and pass it to RecordExplorer as initialPage, preserving client filtering and pagination behavior.

description: Server-render an initial page of public observations on the observations index.

asi.hypothesis: Passing a server-fetched initial image-sighting page into RecordExplorer will make /observations expose real public sighting cards in initial HTML while preserving existing client-side filtering and pagination.
asi.finding: observations_server_content_gaps dropped from 1 to 0 after app/observations/page.tsx started fetching walkOccurrences({ media: "image", target: 24, after: null, resolveMedia: false }) and passing initialPage to RecordExplorer.
asi.tradeoff: The fetch is best-effort and falls back to the existing client-loaded explorer if the indexer request fails; no user-facing copy, robots rules, or metadata text changed.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript validation. Future iterations can consider the same server-rendered first-page pattern for /projects and /organizations, but those clients need cursor-aware initialPage support first to avoid disabling load-more.

name: Improve projects server-rendered crawlable content

description: Baseline projects crawlable-content audit; projects index lacks an initial server-rendered project page.

asi.hypothesis: After metadata, hreflang, sitemap, social previews, CollectionPage JSON-LD, and observation server content are clean, the projects index should expose real project cards and links in initial server HTML.
asi.finding: projects_server_content_gaps baseline is 1 because app/projects/page.tsx renders ProjectsExploreClient without fetching an initial project page, so crawlers/no-JS previews get metadata and JSON-LD but not project card content.
asi.benchmark_note: This measures crawler-visible content depth on an already indexable public listing page; it does not reward hidden text, keyword stuffing, or changing crawler access.
asi.next_action_hint: Add cursor-aware initialPage support to ProjectsExploreClient, fetch the default first featured project page on the server, and pass it into /projects while preserving load-more and filter behavior.

description: Server-render an initial page of public projects on the projects index.

asi.hypothesis: Passing a server-fetched first page of featured projects into ProjectsExploreClient will make /projects expose real project cards and links in initial HTML while preserving existing filtering and pagination.
asi.finding: projects_server_content_gaps dropped from 1 to 0 after app/projects/page.tsx started fetching fetchProjects(48, null, ..., { sort: "newest", featuredBadgesOnly: true }) and passing initialPage to ProjectsExploreClient.
asi.tradeoff: The server fetch is best-effort and falls back to the existing client-loaded explorer if the indexer request fails; ProjectsExploreClient now uses cursor-aware initialPage state so load-more still works and query/filter changes still trigger client fetches.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript validation. Future iterations can consider the same cursor-aware initialPage pattern for /organizations, or ItemList JSON-LD once server-rendered list items are available.

name: Improve organizations server-rendered crawlable content

description: Baseline organizations crawlable-content audit; organizations index lacks an initial server-rendered organization page.

asi.hypothesis: After metadata, hreflang, sitemap, social previews, CollectionPage JSON-LD, and server-rendered observations/projects content are clean, the organizations index should expose real organization cards and profile links in initial server HTML.
asi.finding: organizations_server_content_gaps baseline is 1 because app/organizations/page.tsx renders OrganizationsClient without fetching an initial organization page, so crawlers/no-JS previews get metadata and JSON-LD but not organization card content.
asi.benchmark_note: This measures crawler-visible content depth on an already indexable public listing page; it does not reward hidden text, keyword stuffing, or changing crawler access.
asi.next_action_hint: Add cursor-aware initialPage support to OrganizationsClient, fetch the default first featured organization page on the server, and pass it into /organizations while preserving load-more and filter behavior.

description: Server-render an initial page of public organizations on the organizations index.

asi.hypothesis: Passing a server-fetched first page of featured organizations into OrganizationsClient will make /organizations expose real organization cards and profile links in initial HTML while preserving existing filtering and pagination.
asi.finding: organizations_server_content_gaps dropped from 1 to 0 after app/organizations/page.tsx started fetching fetchSites(24, null, ..., "both", { sort: "newest", featuredBadgesOnly: true }) and passing initialPage to OrganizationsClient.
asi.tradeoff: The server fetch is best-effort and falls back to the existing client-loaded explorer if the indexer request fails; OrganizationsClient now uses cursor-aware initialPage state so load-more still works and query/filter changes still trigger client fetches.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript validation. Future iterations can add ItemList JSON-LD for server-rendered projects/observations/organizations once initial page records are available in all three list pages.

name: Improve list page ItemList structured data

description: Baseline ItemList structured data audit; public list pages do not expose ItemList JSON-LD for server-rendered records.

asi.hypothesis: Now that projects, observations, and organizations all server-render initial records, adding ItemList JSON-LD for those visible records can connect each CollectionPage to the actual listed entities and canonical detail/profile URLs.
asi.finding: list_item_structured_data_gaps baseline is 3 because app/projects/page.tsx, app/observations/page.tsx, and app/organizations/page.tsx emit CollectionPage JSON-LD but no ItemList JSON-LD for their initial records.
asi.benchmark_note: This is standard structured data for visible list content; it should describe the same server-rendered cards and not add hidden keyword text or alter crawler access.
asi.next_action_hint: Add ItemList JSON-LD scripts to the three list pages using each initialPage records array, with canonical project/observation/profile URLs and existing record names/descriptions/images where available.

description: Add ItemList JSON-LD for server-rendered records on public list pages.

asi.hypothesis: ItemList JSON-LD for the same initial records rendered on /projects, /observations, and /organizations will help search engines connect each CollectionPage to its visible listed entities and canonical detail/profile URLs.
asi.finding: list_item_structured_data_gaps dropped from 3 to 0 after adding projects-, observations-, and organizations-item-list-json-ld scripts based on each initialPage records array.
asi.tradeoff: Structured data reuses existing record names/descriptions/images and canonical app URLs; it adds no hidden UI copy, does not change robots/canonical behavior, and emits empty ItemLists if a best-effort initial fetch fails rather than blocking the page.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript validation. Future iterations could inspect dynamic observation detail metadata/structured data or consolidate repeated ItemList helper code if it becomes burdensome.

name: Improve dynamic observation detail SEO metadata

description: Baseline observation detail metadata audit; dynamic sighting pages lack hreflang, canonical social URLs, Twitter card metadata, and Observation JSON-LD.

asi.hypothesis: Observation detail pages are now linked from server-rendered ItemLists and can act as public landing pages, so they should preserve localized alternates, canonical social preview URLs, Twitter card metadata, and structured data describing the sighting.
asi.finding: observation_detail_metadata_gaps baseline is 4: app/observations/[did]/[rkey]/page.tsx uses only a canonical alternate, lacks openGraph.url, lacks Twitter card metadata, and emits no Observation JSON-LD.
asi.benchmark_note: This targets standard metadata and schema.org entity description for existing public detail pages; it does not add hidden keyword text, expose technical identifiers, or alter crawler access.
asi.next_action_hint: Update observation detail generateMetadata to use localizedAlternates(localObservationHref(urlIdentifier, rkey)), add openGraph.url and Twitter summary_large_image metadata, and emit an observation-json-ld script in the page using existing record/owner/location/image fields.

description: Complete dynamic observation detail metadata and structured data.

asi.hypothesis: Observation detail pages linked from server-rendered ItemLists will be clearer public landing pages if they preserve hreflang alternates, canonical social preview URLs, Twitter card metadata, and Observation JSON-LD based on the visible sighting content.
asi.finding: observation_detail_metadata_gaps dropped from 4 to 0 after observation detail metadata switched to localizedAlternates, added Open Graph url and Twitter summary_large_image metadata, and emitted observation-json-ld structured data.
asi.tradeoff: No visible UI copy changed. Structured data reuses existing sighting name, date, location, image, scientific name, and observer profile fields; it avoids adding protocol identifiers as user-facing text and does not change robots/canonical access.
asi.next_action_hint: Run TypeScript validation. Future iterations could add breadcrumb JSON-LD to observation detail pages or sitemap discovery for high-quality public observation detail pages if product strategy supports indexing them broadly.

name: Improve observation detail breadcrumb structured data

description: Baseline observation breadcrumb audit; dynamic sighting pages lack BreadcrumbList JSON-LD.

asi.hypothesis: Dynamic observation detail pages are now richer public landing pages with complete metadata and Observation JSON-LD, so BreadcrumbList JSON-LD can help search engines understand the home → observations → sighting hierarchy.
asi.finding: observation_breadcrumb_gaps baseline is 1 because app/observations/[did]/[rkey]/page.tsx emits Observation JSON-LD but no BreadcrumbList JSON-LD for the page hierarchy.
asi.benchmark_note: BreadcrumbList is standard structured data for search-result hierarchy and does not alter visible UI, keyword density, or crawler access.
asi.next_action_hint: Add buildObservationBreadcrumbJsonLd and emit an observation-breadcrumb-json-ld script using existing localized back label, observation name, and canonical detail URL.
